cards.title: Use-case tip: Your stand-up in 30 seconds

storyboard.title: Jot for your morning stand-up
storyboard.use: Instagram Reel / X video / GIF — developer workflow: Slack compose → hotkey → speak → text appears. Morning routine hook.

shots.visual: Slack DM compose field open, cursor blinking, empty — the classic blank stand-up moment
Hand presses ⌥Space on keyboard; Dynamic Island-style pill appears under Mac notch
Pill shows pulsing audio waveform; user speaks stand-up update aloud (what I did, doing, blockers)
Full stand-up text appears in Slack compose; pill vanishes; send button visible; jot-transcribe.com watermark lower-right

drafts.text:
My morning stand-up was 90 seconds of typing the same things: what I did, what I'm doing, any blockers.

Now I press ⌥Space, say it once in 20 seconds, and it pastes straight into Slack.

Exact same update. No typing. Every morning.

What parts of your day are you still typing when you could be speaking?

#macOS #productivity #indiedev
drafts.note: Attach standup-tip.svg (export as 1080×1080 PNG). Do NOT put the link in the post body — LinkedIn downranks posts with external links. Paste the utm_url as the FIRST COMMENT immediately after posting. The closing question is the engagement trigger — answer every reply.

drafts.text: My stand-up update used to take 90 seconds of typing. Now: ⌥Space → speak → paste. 20 seconds. Jot. Free. On-device. Open source.
drafts.note: Attach standup-tip.svg (export as PNG). No link in post — post the utm_url as the first reply immediately after posting. ~131 characters, within 200 limit.

drafts.text:
I started dictating my morning stand-up instead of typing it — here's what changed

I have the same stand-up every morning: what I did yesterday, what I'm doing today, any blockers. For months I'd open Slack and type it out — usually 60–90 seconds of the exact same words.

Three weeks ago I started dictating it instead. Press a hotkey, say it once, it pastes into Slack. The whole thing is 20 seconds now.

I built Jot for this (and for everything else I used to type, honestly). Transcription is entirely on-device on the Apple Neural Engine — no cloud, no account, no audio leaves the Mac. The hotkey works in any app: Slack, email, your code editor, anywhere.

Some patterns I noticed after 3 weeks:
- Stand-up updates are now 3–4 sentences instead of 2 bullet points. I'm more descriptive when I speak.
- I don't dread the stand-up window anymore — the friction dropped from 'ugh I have to type this again' to 'done already'.
- I started dictating other repeated things: Slack replies, commit messages, PR descriptions.

The one limit: Jot handles ~150 words comfortably per take. A full stand-up fits easily, but for a longer email you'd break it into chunks.

Apple Silicon only, macOS 14+. Free and open source.

(I built this — disclosure)

Link in comments if you want to try it.

notes: Downloads reached 151 (+9 from June 17's 142, avg7=8/day). The HN-driven spike of +14 on June 17 is settling toward the ~8/day baseline — healthy sustained discovery. v1.14.1 now at 32 downloads, the most-downloaded release by a wide margin. iTunes API returned 403 for the 4th consecutive day; appstore.json preserved unchanged (ratingCount: 3, avgRating: 5.0, installs: 0). No actionable Reddit/HN reply threads found with URLs today; the DEV Community 'godnick' account is an algorithmic SEO content farm, not a real author. Superwhisper pricing discussion ($249.99/$849 lifetime) continues in search results but no thread URLs surfaced. Outreach window reopens June 19 — priority targets: Resonant (onresonant.com) and the speakmac.app/blog team (publishes Mac dictation comparison roundups ranking in search). Reddit recommended:false (last true June 15, earliest June 19). Instagram recommended:false (last true June 17, earliest June 20). Today's stand-up use-case angle plays directly to the Show HN developer audience who installed Jot this week — giving them a concrete daily habit is a retention hook.
